retry_reason: The kref_init operation never fails; the only failure path is allocation failure (returning NULL), which may be retryable under memory pressure but is not a refcount failure.
contract_summary: Creation (set) wrapper: allocates and initializes a reference-counted struct aa_policydb with an initial reference count of 1 via kref_init. On success, the caller receives a fully initialized object with one reference already held. The caller must release that reference with a corresponding put (e.g., aa_put_policydb) when the object is no longer needed. Failure returns NULL — the caller should check the return value and handle allocation failure appropriately.
